text: Boucher states that “Morphological studies of normal cultures under these steady-state conditions reveal that the 7 µm height is optimal for the extension and beating of cilia and, therefore, is physiologically suited to efficient mucociliary clearance.” (Boucher R., 2003). The link between decreased ASL height and reduced cilia beating has been established in multiple in vitro and in vivo studies (Van Goor et al., 2009; Xu et al., 2015; Zhang et al., 2014), and even though the evidence does not describe causality between these two events, this KER is biologically plausible (Button et al., 2012; Mall, 2008).
